title: Ethena Labs to Allocate $250M to Securitize’s Tokenized AAA CLO Fund on Solana
summary: Ethena Labs is allocating $250 million to Securitize’s tokenized AAA-rated collateralized loan obligation (CLO) fund, which is deploying on the Solana blockchain. This move brings institutional-grade structured credit to the blockchain, offering floating-rate returns.
body:
Ethena Labs, a prominent player in the crypto space, has announced a $250 million allocation to Securitize’s tokenized AAA-rated collateralized loan obligation (CLO) fund. This fund, known as STAC, is one of Securitize’s in-house offerings and provides on-chain access to institutional-grade, floating-rate structured credit. The deployment of STAC on the Solana blockchain marks a notable step in bringing traditional financial products to the blockchain.

The STAC fund is designed to offer investors exposure to high-quality, floating-rate structured credit, which typically adjusts with market interest rates. This allocation by Ethena Labs underscores the growing interest in tokenizing traditional financial instruments, making them accessible to a broader range of investors. The use of the Solana blockchain is expected to provide scalability and efficiency for these transactions.

For everyday investors, this development could mean easier access to sophisticated financial products that were previously only available to institutional investors. The tokenization of these assets on the blockchain can reduce barriers to entry, potentially offering higher returns and greater liquidity. However, it’s important for investors to understand the risks associated with structured credit and the volatility of the crypto market.

This allocation is part of a broader trend of bringing traditional financial products onto the blockchain. As more institutions and companies like Ethena Labs and Securitize explore this space, we can expect to see more tokenized assets and innovative financial products. Investors should keep an eye on the performance of STAC and similar offerings, as well as any regulatory developments that could impact this growing sector.
twitterThread: Ethena Labs is allocating $250M to Securitize’s tokenized AAA-rated CLO fund on Solana. This brings institutional-grade structured credit to the blockchain.
The STAC fund offers floating-rate returns, adjusting with market interest rates. It’s a big step in tokenizing traditional financial products for broader access.

This trend could open up high-quality investments to everyday users. But remember: crypto volatility and structured credit risks still apply. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-14-ethena-labs-to-invest-250m-in-securitizes-tokenized-aaa-clo-fund-on-solana
standaloneTweet: Ethena Labs is allocating $250M to a tokenized AAA-rated CLO fund on Solana, making high-grade structured credit accessible on the blockchain. Could this be the future of institutional investing for everyday users?
